workflow.name: Extract passport data with OpenAI OCR and generate QR codes
workflow.description:
### Overview
This workflow processes passport images submitted through a form, extracts structured data using OpenAI OCR, and generates QR codes with the extracted information. Results are displayed on the form completion page and sent via email.

The workflow reduces manual data entry and speeds up document processing workflows where passport information needs to be captured consistently. It works best with clear, front-facing passport images.

### How it works
1. User submits a form with one or more passport images
2. Files are split and processed individually
3. Each file is validated as an image and resized for OCR accuracy
4. Image is converted to base64 and sent to OpenAI for data extraction
5. Extracted fields are cleaned, standardized, and validated
6. QR payload is generated from standardized data and converted to QR code image URL
7. Results are aggregated into HTML summary
8. Summary is displayed on form completion page and sent via email

### Setup steps
- Add OpenAI API credentials to the OCR request node
- Connect your Gmail account for email delivery
- Replace YOUR_EMAIL placeholder with recipient address
- Test with 1–3 clear passport images

### Customization
- Modify OCR prompt to support other document types
- Adjust QR payload format to match your system requirements

workflow.name: Extract passport data with OpenAI and create QR codes

parameters.formDescription: Upload multiple passport images and generate QR codes

parameters.jsCode:
let x = $('Loop Over images').first();
console.log('x...', x)
console.log('input...', $input.first())
const binBuffer = await this.helpers.getBinaryDataBuffer(0, 'data');
console.log('binBuffer...', binBuffer)
x.json.imageBase64 = Buffer.from(binBuffer).toString('base64')
x.binary = $input.first().binary
return x

parameters.content:
## Result aggregation
Combine extracted data and QR codes, then deliver via form completion page and email

## Image preprocessing
Validate image files, resize for optimal OCR accuracy, prepare for data extraction

## Form input processing
Receive multiple image uploads from form, split into individual items for sequential processing

## Overview
This workflow extracts structured passport data using OpenAI OCR and generates QR codes from the extracted information.

### How it works
1. User submits passport images via form
2. Each image is validated and resized
3. OpenAI extracts passport fields
4. Data is standardized and validated
5. QR codes are generated
6. Results delivered via form and email

### Setup
- Add OpenAI API credentials to OCR node
- Connect Gmail for email delivery
- Replace YOUR_EMAIL with your address
- Test with clear passport images

### Customization
- Modify OCR prompt for other documents
- Adjust QR format for your system
⚠️ Privacy: Personal identity data. Limit access and configure email carefully.

## Data extraction and QR generation
Send images to OpenAI OCR, standardize extracted fields, validate results, create QR codes

user.name: ICTS Automation
user.bio:
ICTS Automation provides business process automation for SMEs, helping companies reduce manual work, minimize errors, and boost productivity. We design and deploy workflow automation solutions using tools like n8n, Microsoft Power Automate, and custom AI bots that fit your existing systems.

Our goal is to help small and medium businesses simplify operations, save time, and focus on growth instead of repetitive tasks.

data.details:
The Edit Image node allows you to manipulate and edit images. Use this node when you want to:

- Blur an image
- Add a border to an image
- Create a new image
- Crop an image
- Composite an image on top of another
- Draw on an image
- Get information about the image
- Rotate an image
- Change the size of an image
- Shear an image along the X or Y axis
- Add text to the image

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
